center.name: 瓦努科大区
center.summary: 瓦努科大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括秘鲁盘尾蜂鸟、秘鲁斑尾蜂鸟、紫领蜂鸟等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Peru it also occurs in the upper Marañón River valley (hence its alternate English name) and very locally on the eastern Andean slope in Huánuco Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: peruviana is found in central and southern Peru, from Huánuco to Junín and Cuzco

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: leucopygia is found in northern Peru in southern Amazonas, San Martín, and Huánuco departments. The valley of the Marañón River separates the two subspecies.{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in central and southern Peru from the Department of Huánuco south into the Department of Puno; it might also occur in the western part of the Department of Ucayali

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The cinnamon-faced tyrannulet is found in a narrow band along the eastern slope of the Andes from Peru's Department of Huánuco south to southwestern Beni Department in Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: poliophrys: Andes of central and southern Peru from the Department of Huánuco to the Department of Puno The grey-browed brushfinch inhabits the interior and edges of humid montane forest and secondary forest. It ranges in elevation between in Venezuela, between in Colombia, between in the northern part of Ecuador, and between and locally much lower in the southern part.{{cite book | last1 =McMullan | first1 =Miles | last2 =Donegan | first2 =Thomas M

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the north it is found in the departments of Huánuco and Pasco and in the south the department of Junín

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: boliviana is found in north-central Peru's San Martín and Huánuco departments and then from the Urubamba Valley in southern Cuzco south on the eastern Andean slope into the western part of central Bolivia's Santa Cruz Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The subspecies of Lafresnaye's piculet are found thus: *Picumnus lafresnayi lafresnayi, from southeastern Colombia south through eastern Ecuador to Peru's Department of San Martín *Picumnus lafresnayi punctifrons, northern Peru's Amazonas Department *Picumnus lafresnayi taczanowskii, northeastern and north-central Peru between the departments of Huánuco and Ayacucho *Picumnus lafresnayi pusillus, north-central Brazil along the middle Amazon east to the Rio Negro Lafresnaye's piculet inhabits the edges and clearings of humid mature forest and also secondary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Maranon thrush is found in the upper and middle basins of the Marañón River from extreme southern Ecuador into northern Peru to Huánuco Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The neblina tapaculo is found in the Eastern Cordillera of Peru's northern Andes, roughly between southern Amazonas and central Huánuco Departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: jelskii of the ocellated piculet is found in central Peru between the departments of Huánuco and Cuzco

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | title =The Birds of Ecuador: Field Guide | publisher =Cornell University Press | volume = II| date =2001 | location =Ithaca | pages =570 | isbn =978-0-8014-8721-7 }} In Peru it is found in southern Amazonas and northern San Martín departments and separately from southern Huánuco to northern Junín departments.{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: spectabilis: from far southern Colombia's Putumayo Department south through eastern Ecuador into northern Peru as far as Huánuco Department The orange-billed sparrow primarily inhabits the understory of humid primary and secondary evergreen forest in the tropical zone.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: yessupi is found in east-central Peru's departments of Huánuco and Pasco and into southwestern Brazil at least to the Rio Juruá

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: moderatus is found in the Andes from the Department of Huánuco in Peru south and east into central Bolivia. The striped treehunter inhabits montane evergreen forest in the subtropical zone and highly favors thickets of Chusquea bamboo, though it also occurs locally in secondary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: huancabambae is found in the Andes of northern Peru from Piura and Cajamarca departments south to Ancash and Huánuco

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of the yellow-breasted warbling antbird is found in the Andean foothills of east-central Peru between the departments of Huánuco and Cuzco

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: aureiventris: eastern slope of the Andes in Peru from Huánuco Department south to Puno Department The ornate flycatcher inhabits humid montane forest, mature secondary forest, and woodlands in the Andean foothills

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: similis is found in central Peru between the departments of Huánuco and Junín. The chestnut-backed antshrike inhabits a variety of landscapes most of which are somewhat open

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ash-colored tapaculo is found in all three Andean ranges in Colombia and south through Ecuador to Peru's departments of Huánuco and Pasco

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in the east Andean foothills of Huánuco, Pasco, Cuzco, and Puno departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Peruvian racket-tail is found in eastern Ecuador and northeastern Peru as far as Huánuco Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: megalura is found in northern Peru from Cajamarca south to Huánuco
